questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the function $f(x)=2 x^{3}-9 a x^{2}+12 a^{2} x+1$ attains its maximum and minimum at $p$ and $q$ respectively such that $p^{2}=q$, then $a$ equals
questions.solution:
Given, $f(x)=2 x^{3}-9 a x^{2}+12 a^{2} x+1$ attains maximum and minimum at $p$ and $q$ respectively. $\therefore$
$$
f^{\prime}(p)=0, f^{\prime}(q)=0
$$
Now,
$$
\begin{array}{c}
f^{\prime \prime}(p) < 0 \text { and } f^{\prime \prime}(q)>0 \\
f^{\prime}(p)=0
\end{array}
$$
and
$$
\begin{array}{r}
f^{\prime}(q)=0 \\
6 p^{2}-18 a p+12 a^{2}=0 \\
6 q^{2}-18 a q+12 a^{2}=0
\end{array}
$$
and
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & p^{2}-3 a p+2 a^{2}=0 \\
\text { and } & q^{2}-3 a q+2 a^{2}=0
\end{array}
$$
$p=a, 2 a, q=a, 2 a$
$\begin{array}{lrl}\Rightarrow & p=a, 2 a, q=a, \\ \text { Now, } & f^{\prime \prime}(p) < 0\end{array}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 12 p-18 a < 0$
$\Rightarrow \quad p < \frac{3}{2} a$
and $f^{\prime \prime}(q)>0 \Rightarrow 12 q-18 a>0$
$\Rightarrow \quad q>\frac{3}{2} a$
From Eqs. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get $\begin{aligned} p &=a, q=2 a \\ \text { Now, } \quad p^{2} &=q \end{aligned}$
$$
\begin{array}{lc}
\Rightarrow & a^{2}=2 a \\
\Rightarrow & a=0,2
\end{array}
$$
But for $a=0, f(x)=2 x^{3}+1$ which does not
attains a maximum or minimum for any value of $x$. Hence, $a=2$.
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The maximum value of function $x^{3}-12 x^{2}+36 x+17$ in the interval $[1,10]$ is
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=x^{3}-12 x^{2}+36 x+17$
$\therefore f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^{2}-24 x+36=0$
For maxima, put $f^{\prime}(x)=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad 3 x^{2}-24 x+36=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad(x-2)(x-6)=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad x=2,6$
Again, $f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 x-24$ is negative at $x=2$ So that, $f(6)=17, f(2)=49$
At the end points, $f(1)=42, f(10)=177$ So that, $f(x)$ has its maximum value 177 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\mathrm{f}(x)=x \mathrm{e}^{x(1-x)}, x \in \mathrm{R}$, then $\mathrm{f}(x)$ is
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{f}(x) & =x \mathrm{e}^{x(1-x)} \\
\therefore \quad \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(x) & =x \mathrm{e}^{x(1-x)}[x(-1)+(1-x)]+\mathrm{e}^{x(1-x)} \\
& =\mathrm{e}^{x(1-x)}\left(x-2 x^2+1\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

For $\mathrm{f}(x)$ to be increasing, $\mathrm{f}^{\prime}(x) \geq 0$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^{x(1-x)}\left(x-2 x^2+1\right) \geq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow x-2 x^2+1 \geq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 2 x^2-x-1 \leq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow(2 x+1)(x-1) \leq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow x \in\left[-\frac{1}{2}, 1\right]
\end{aligned}
$$
For $\mathrm{f}(x)$ to be decreasing, $\mathrm{f}^{\prime}(x) \leq 0$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow(2 x+1)(x-1) \geq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow x \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{2}\right] \cup[1, \infty)
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: On the interval $[0,1]$ the function $x^{25}(1-x)^{75}$ takes its maximum value at the point
questions.solution:
Given, $f(x)=x^{25}(1-x)^{75}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x) &=25 x^{24}(1-x)^{75}-75 x^{25}(1-x)^{74} \\
&=25 x^{24}(1-x)^{74}(1-4 x) \\
\therefore \quad f^{\prime}(x) &=0 \\
\Rightarrow \quad x &=0,1,1 / 4
\end{aligned}
$$
If $x < 1 / 4$, then
$$
f^{\prime}(x)=25 x^{24}(1-x)^{74}(1-4 x)>0 .
$$
and if $x>1 / 4$, then
$$
f^{\prime}(x)=25 x^{24}(1-x)^{74}(1-4 x) < 0 .
$$
Thus, $f^{\prime}(x)$ changes its sign from positive to negative as $x$ passes through $1 / 4$ from left to right. Hence, $f(x)$ attains its maximum at $x=1 / 4$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a triangle, the sum of lengths of two sides is $x$ and the product of the lengths of the same two sides is $y$. If $x^2-\mathrm{c}^2-y$, where $\mathrm{c}$ is the length of the third side of the triangle, then the circumradius of the triangle is
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{a}$ and $\mathrm{b}$ be the lengths of two sides of $\mathrm{a}$ triangle.
$\therefore \quad$ According to the given condition,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}=x \text { and } \mathrm{ab}=y \\
& \therefore \quad x^2-\mathrm{c}^2=y \Rightarrow(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})^2-\mathrm{c}^2=\mathrm{ab} \\
& \Rightarrow a^2+b^2+2 a b-c^2=a b \\
& \Rightarrow a^2+b^2-c^2=-a b \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2-\mathrm{c}^2}{2 \mathrm{ab}}=-\frac{1}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \cos \mathrm{C}=\frac{1}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{C}=\frac{2 \pi}{3} \\
& \Rightarrow \text { circumradius }=\frac{\mathrm{c}}{2 \sin \mathrm{C}}=\frac{\mathrm{c}}{2 \sin \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)}=\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\sqrt{3}} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math>m ladder leans against a vertical wall. If the top of the ladder begins to slide down the wall at the rate <math><mn>25</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></math> , then the rate (in cm/sec.) at which the bottom of the ladder slides away from the wall on the horizontal ground when the top of the ladder is <math><mn>1</mn></math> m above the ground is:
questions.solution:
dydt=-25 cm/sec , dxdt=?
Now, x2+y2=22=4
differentiating w.r.t. t both side
2xdxdt+2ydydt=0 x2+y2=4 when y=1 ⇒x2=3⇒x=3
⇒dxdt=-yxdydt
⇒dxdt 3,1=-13×-25=253 cm/sec
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A kite is $120 \mathrm{~m}$ high and $130 \mathrm{~m}$ of string is out. If the kite is moving away horizontally at the rate of $39 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$, then the rate at which the string is being out, is
questions.solution: Let 'P' be the position of the kite and PR be the string. Let $\mathrm{QR}=x$ and $\mathrm{PR}=y$ By Pythagoras theorem, $\begin{aligned} & -\mathrm{PR}^2=\mathrm{PQ}^2+\mathrm{QR}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow y^2=(120)^2+x^2... (i) \end{aligned}$ Differentiating w.r.t.t, we get $\begin{aligned} & 2 y \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{dt}}=2 x \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\mathrm{dt}} \\ & \Rightarrow y \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{dt}}=x \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\mathrm{dt}}... (ii) \end{aligned}$ Now, kite is moving away horizontally at the rate of $39 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. $\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad & \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\mathrm{dt}}=39 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec} \\ & \text { From (i), } \\ & (130)^2=(120)^2+x^2 \\ \Rightarrow & x^2=16900-14400 \\ \Rightarrow & x^2=2500 \\ \Rightarrow & x=50 \\ & \text { From (ii), } \\ & 130 \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{dt}}=50 \times 39 \\ \therefore \quad & \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{50 \times 39}{130}=15 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The maximum value of <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">log</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi> </mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></math> is
questions.solution:
fx=log⁡xx
f ′ (x)= x 1 x −logx x 2 = 1−logx x 2
f ′ ( x )=0⇒ 1−logx x 2 =0
⇒log⁡x=1  
⇒x=e
⇒ Max value is fe=log⁡ee=1e

questions.q: Let y be the number of people in a village at time t. Assume that the rate of change of the population is proportional to the number of people in the village at any time and further assume that the population never increase in time. Then, the population of the village at any fixed <math><mi>t</mi></math> is given by
questions.solution:
Given, dydt∝y,where y is the position of village

⇒1ydy=k dt

⇒   log⁡y=log⁡c+kt    [on integrating]

⇒    log⁡yc=kt⇒y=cekt

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the interval $(-3,3)$ the function $f(x)=\frac{x}{3}+\frac{3}{x}, x \neq 0$ is :
questions.solution:
$\because \quad f(x)=\frac{x}{3}+\frac{3}{x}$
$f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{3}-\frac{3}{x^2}$
It is clear that $f^{\prime}(x)$ is less than zero in the interval $(-3,3)$.
Thus $f(x)$ is decreasing in the interval $(-3,3)$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Divide 10 into two parts such that the sum of double of the first and the square of the second is minimum
questions.solution:
Let $x$ and $y$ be the two parts of the number 10 .
$\therefore$
$x+y=10\ldots$ (i)
According to the question, Let $\quad \begin{aligned} A &=2 x+y^{2} \\ &=2 x+(10-x)^{2} \\ &=2 x+100+x^{2}-20 x \\ &=x^{2}-18 x+100 \end{aligned}$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get $\frac{d A}{d x}=2 x-18$
For max or min of $A$, Put $\quad \frac{d A}{d x}=0=2 x-18$
$\Rightarrow$
$x=9$
Now, $\quad \frac{d^{2} A}{d x^{2}}=2>0 \quad(\min )$
On putting $x=9$ in Eq. (i), we get $y=1$
$\therefore \quad(x, y)=(9,1)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>R</mi><mo>*</mo></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>-</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>/</mo><mi>k</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> The function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>R</mi><mo>*</mo></msup><mo>→</mo><mi>R</mi></math> is defined as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
Given:fx=tanx-x

Differentiating on both sides, we get

f'x=sec2x-1

As, secx∈(-∞,-1]∪[1,∞)

∴sec2x∈[1,∞)

∴sec2x-1≥0

∴f'x≥0 for all x∈R*

∴fx is an increasing function.

Hence, option 1 is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of real roots of the equation $e^{x^1}+\log x+x-2=0, x \neq 0$, is
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
e^{x-1}+\log x+x-2=0
$$
Let $\quad f(x)=e^{x-1}+\log x+x-2$
$$
f^{\prime}(x)=e^{x-1}+\frac{1}{x}+1
$$
$$
f^{\prime}(x)>0, \forall x \in R
$$
$f(x)$ is monotonic increasing function.
Hence, $f(x)$ has only one real roots.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The height of right circular cylinder of maximum volume inscribed in a sphere of diameter $2 a$ is
questions.solution:
Let the radius and height of the cylinder are and $h$, respectively.
In $\triangle A O M$ $\therefore$ $$ \begin{array}{l} r^{2}+\left(\frac{h^{2}}{4}\right)=a^{2} \\ h^{2}=4\left(a^{2}-r^{2}\right) \end{array} $$ Now, $V=\pi r^{2} h=\pi\left(a^{2} h-\frac{1}{4} h^{3}\right)$ For max or min, $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{d V}{d h} &=\pi\left(a^{2}-\frac{3}{4} h^{2}\right)=0 \\ \Rightarrow & h &=\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}\right) a \end{aligned} $$ Now, $\quad \frac{d^{2} V}{d h^{2}}=-\frac{6 h}{4} < 0$ So, $V$ is maximum at $h=\frac{2 a}{\sqrt{3}}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If a cylindrical vessel of given volume $V$ with no lid on the top is to be made from a sheet of metal, then the radius $(r)$ and height $(h)$ of the vessel so that the metal sheet used is minimum, is
questions.solution:
We have given, $V=$ volume of cylindrical vessel $r=$ radius and $h=$ height
As we know for cylindrical vessel $$ v=\pi r^2 h $$
Let $S$ be the area of metal sheet used to form a cylindrical vessel. Then, $$ \begin{aligned} & S=2 \pi r h+\pi r^2=2 \pi r \frac{V}{\pi r^2}+\pi r^2 \quad \text { [using Eq. }(i) \text { ] } \\ & \Rightarrow \quad S(r)=\frac{2 V}{r}+\pi r^2 \end{aligned} $$ For minimum value of $S(r), S^{\prime}(r)=0$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{-2 V}{r}+2 \pi r=0 \Rightarrow \frac{2 V}{r^2}=2 \pi r \\ & \Rightarrow r^3=\frac{V}{\pi} \Rightarrow r=\sqrt[3]{\frac{V}{\pi}} \end{aligned} $$ Now, by using the value of $r$ in Eq. ( $i)$ $$ \begin{aligned} V & =\pi\left(\frac{V}{\pi}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}} \cdot h \\ \Rightarrow \quad & h=\frac{V}{\pi} \times \frac{\pi^{2 / 3}}{v^{2 / 3}} \Rightarrow h=\frac{V^{1 / 3}}{\pi^{1 / 3}}=\sqrt[3]{\frac{V}{\pi}} \end{aligned} $$ Hence, the required radius is $r=\sqrt[3]{\frac{V}{\pi}}$ and height is $$ h=\sqrt[3]{\frac{V}{\pi}} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find the minimum value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi></math> when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn></math>.
questions.solution:
Given: xy=6

⇒y=6x

⇒fx,y=2x+3y

⇒fx=2x+3×6x

⇒fx=2x+18x

⇒f'x=2-18x2

Put f'x=0 for maximum or minimum

⇒0=2-18x2

⇒x2=9

⇒x=±3

⇒f''x=0--18×2x3

⇒f''3=3633>0

⇒f''(3)>0

At x=3 ,fx is minimum

The minimum value of f3=23+32 =12.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A container is the shape of an inverted cone. Its height is $6 \mathrm{~m}$ and radius is $4 \mathrm{~m}$ at the top. If it is filled with water at the rate of $3 \mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{min}$ then the rate of change of height of water (in $\mathrm{mt} / \mathrm{min}$ ) when the water level is $3 \mathrm{~m}$, is
questions.solution:
Let $V$ be the volume, $r$ be the radius and $h$ be the height of an inverted cone at any time $t$. Then
Given, $\frac{d v}{d t}=3 \mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{min}$ We know that,
From similarity of triangle $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{B O}{B A}=\frac{O D}{A C} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{6}{h} \quad \frac{4}{r} \Rightarrow r=\frac{2}{3} h \end{aligned} $$ From Eq. (i), $$ V=\frac{1}{3} \pi\left(\frac{2}{3} h\right)^2 h=\frac{4}{27} \pi h^3 $$ On differentiating w.r.t. $t$, we get $$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d v}{d t}=\frac{4}{27} \pi 3 h^2 \frac{d h}{d t} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 3=\frac{4}{27} \pi 3 h^2 \frac{d h}{d t} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d h}{d t}=\frac{3 \times 27}{4 \pi 3 h^2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{d h}{d t}\right)_{h=3}=\frac{3 \times 27}{4 \pi 27}=\frac{3}{4 \pi} \mathrm{mt} / \mathrm{min} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The condition $f(x)=x^3+p x^2+q x+r(x \in R)$ to have no extreme value, is
questions.solution:
Given, $f(x)=x^3+p x^2+q x+r$
Now, $f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2+2 x p+q$
Clearly $\quad f^{\prime}(x)>0$
Now, $\quad b^2-4 a c < 0$
$\Rightarrow \quad 4 p^2-4 \times 3 \times q < 0$
$\begin{array}{rrr}\Rightarrow & 4 p^2-12 q < 0 \\ \Rightarrow & p^2 < 3 q\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $f(x)=p x^3+q x^2+r x+t$ attains local minimum and local maximum values at $x=-2$ and $x=2$ respectively and $p$ is a root of $9 x^2-1=0$, then $\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{q}+\mathrm{r}=$
questions.solution:
$\because f^{\prime}(x)=3 p x^2+2 q x+r$  ...(i)
Since, $f(x)$ attain local minimum and maximum at $x=-2$ and $x=2$ respectively.
$\therefore f^{\prime}(x)=k(x+2)(x-2)=k\left(x^2-4\right)$ ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii)
$\begin{aligned} & k\left(x^2-4\right)=3 p x^2+2 q x+r \\ & \Rightarrow \quad k x^2-4 k=3 p x^2+2 q x+r\end{aligned}$
Comparing both sides, we get
$k=3 p, q=0$ and $r=-4 k$
Now, $f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 p x+2 q=6 p x \quad\{\because q=0\}$
$\Rightarrow f^{\prime \prime}(-2)=-12 p>0 \quad\{\because x=-2$ is local minimum $\}$
$\Rightarrow p < 0$
$\because p$ is a root of $9 x^2-1=0 \Rightarrow x= \pm \frac{1}{3}$
$\therefore p < 0 \Rightarrow p=-\frac{1}{3}$
So, $k=3 \times\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)=-1$
$\Rightarrow r=-4 k=-4(-1)=4$
$\therefore p+q+r=-\frac{1}{3}+0+4=\frac{11}{3}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ladder of length $17 \mathrm{~m}$ rests with one end against a vertical wall and the other on the level ground. If the lower end slips away at the rate of $1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$., then when it is $8 \mathrm{~m}$ away from the wall, its upper end is coming down at the rate of
questions.solution: In $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}, \mathrm{AC}$ represents ladder $\mathrm{AB} \rightarrow$ vertical wall Let $\mathrm{AB}=x, \mathrm{BC}=y$ $\therefore \quad \angle \mathrm{ABC}=90^{\circ}$ By Pythagoras theorem, $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{AB}^2+\mathrm{BC}^2=\mathrm{AC}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2+y^2=17^2 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2=289-y^2 ... (i)\\ & \Rightarrow x^2=289-64 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2=225 \\ & \Rightarrow x=15 \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned}$ Consider equation (i), $x^2=289-y^2$ Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get $\begin{aligned} & 2 x \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\mathrm{dt}}=-2 y \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{dt}} \\ & \Rightarrow 15 \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\mathrm{dt}}=-8(1) \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{-8}{15} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$ Negative sign shows that the ladder is moving down. i.e., vertical length is decreasing $\therefore \quad$ Upper end is coming down at the rate of $\frac{8}{15} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Air is discharging from a large spherical balloon at the rate of 4 cubic meters per minute. Then, the rate at which the surface area is shrinking when the radius of the balloon is $8 \mathrm{~m}$, is
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The minimum value of $\left(1+\frac{1}{\sin ^n \alpha}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{\cos ^n \alpha}\right)$ is
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=\left(1+\frac{1}{\sin ^n x}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{\cos ^n x}\right)$
$=\left(1+\operatorname{cosec}^n x\right)\left(1+\sec ^n x\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\left[-\mathrm{n} \operatorname{cosec}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{x}-\operatorname{cosec} n \cdot \cot \mathrm{x}\right] \mathrm{dx} \\ & \left(1+\sec ^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{x}\right)+\left(1+\operatorname{cosec}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{x}\right) \\ & \operatorname{nsec}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{x} \times \sec \mathrm{x} \tan \mathrm{x}=0\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{4}$
Now, $\mathrm{f}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\left[1+(2)^{\mathrm{n} / 2}\right]^2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\log (1+x)-\frac{2 x}{2+x}$ is increasing, then
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=\log (1+x)-\frac{2 x}{2+x^2}$
$$
f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{(1+x)}-\frac{2}{2+x}+\frac{2 x}{(2+x)^2}
$$
For increasing function, $f^{\prime}(x)>0$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow \quad(2+x)^2-2(2+x)(1+x)+2 x(1+x) & >0 \\
\Rightarrow 4+x^2+4 x-4-6 x-2 x^2+2 x+2 x^2 & >0 \\
x^2 & >0
\end{aligned}
$$
This shows $x$ lies between $-\infty$ to $\infty$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\theta$ is the acute angle between the curves $x^2+y^2=4$ and $y^2=3 x$ then $\tan \theta=$
questions.solution:
Given curves are $x^2+y^2=4$ and $y^2=3 x$ Put the value of $y^2$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow x^2+3 x-4=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x^2+4 x-x-4=0 \\
& x(x+4)-1(x+4)=0 \\
& (x-1)(x+4)=0 \\
& x-1=0, x+4=0 \\
& x=1, x=-4
\end{aligned}
$$
Put in $y^2=3 \mathrm{x}$, then, $y^2=3, y= \pm \sqrt{3}$. $(x, y) \rightarrow(1, \sqrt{3}) \&(1, \sqrt{3})$
Now, Differentiate both curves w.r.t $x$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow x^2+y^2=4 \\
& 2 x+2 y \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=0 \\
& y \frac{d y}{d x}=-x \\
& \frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x}{y} \\
& \Rightarrow y=\sqrt{3 x} \\
& \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2 \sqrt{x}}
\end{aligned}
$$
Put the point $(1, \sqrt{3})$ in their differentiation.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& m_1=\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{3}}, m_2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\
& \tan \theta=\left|\frac{m_1-m_2}{1+m_1 m_2}\right|=\left|\frac{-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}{1-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}\right|=\left|\frac{\frac{-2-3}{2 \sqrt{3}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right| \\
& \tan \theta=\left|\frac{-5}{\sqrt{3}}\right|=\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}
\end{aligned}
$$
So, $\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}$ is the correct option.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $f:[2,3] \rightarrow R$ is defined by $f(x)=x^3+3 x-2$, then the range $f(x)$ is contained in the interval
questions.solution:
Given, $f(x)=x^3+3 x-2$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$\begin{aligned}
& \quad f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2+3 \\
& \text { Put } \quad f^{\prime}(x)=0 \Rightarrow 3 x^2+3=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x^2=-1 \\
& \therefore \quad f(x) \text { is either increasing or decreasing. } \\
& \text { At } x=2, f(2)=2^3+3(2)-2=12 \\
& \text { At } x=3, f(3)=3^3+3(3)-2=34 \\
& \therefore f(x) \in[12,34] .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Define $f(x)=\frac{1}{2}[|\sin x|+\sin x], 0 < x \leq 2 \pi$. Then, $f$ is
questions.solution:
Given,
$$
f(x)=\frac{1}{2}[|\sin x|+\sin x], 0 < x \leq 2 \pi
$$
Case I, when, $0 < x \leq \pi$
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x) & =\frac{1}{2}[\sin x+\sin x]=\sin x \\
f^{\prime}(x) & =\cos x \\
\cos x & >0, \text { for } 0 < x < \frac{\pi}{2} \text { (increasing) } \\
\cos x & < 0, \text { for } \frac{\pi}{2} < x < \pi \text { (decreasing) }
\end{aligned}
$$
Case II When $\pi < x \leq 2 \pi$
$$
f(x)=\frac{1}{2}[-\sin x+\sin x]=0
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> are points of extrema of <math> <mrow> <mtext>f</mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>=</mo> <mi>α</mi> <mtext> log </mtext> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>x</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>+</mo> <mi>β</mi> <msup> <mtext>x</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <mtext>x</mtext> </mrow><mo>,</mo> </math> then
questions.solution:
f x = α log x + βx 2 + x

If x<0

f⁡ x = α log - x + β x 2 + x

∴    f⁡ ′ x = - α - x + 2 β x + 1

If x>0

f⁡ x = α log x + β x 2 + x

∴    f⁡ ′ x = α x + 2 β x + 1

f⁡ ′ - 1 = - α - 2 β + 1 = 0

f⁡ ′ 2 = α 2 + 4 β + 1 = 0

2 f⁡ ′ - 1 = - 2 α - 4 β + 2 = 0

and f⁡ ′ 2 = α 2 + 4 β + 1 = 0

Adding,

- 3 α 2 + 3 = 0

∴    α = 2

∴    β = - 1 2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution:
∵   fx=a-x-38/9

∴    f 'x=0-89x-3-1/9

At  x=3, f 'x is not defined.

hence sign scheme of f 'x is



∴fx is maximum at x=3

questions.q: If <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is the largest interval in which the function <math><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is decreasing, then <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to (where, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mo>.</mo></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer function)
questions.solution:
f ′ x =3 x 2 +12x+a
if fx is decreasing in −3,−1,f'x<0, ∀x∈−3,−1
∴ f ′ −3 = f ′ −1 =0
⇒a3=-3-1
⇒a=9 (using product of roots)

questions.q: The set of values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> for which local extremum values of the function <math><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo></math> are positive where <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi></math> and maxima occurs at <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
f′x=2a2 x2-5ax+3=ax-1 2ax-3=0

x=1a, 32a

If a>0, then local maxima occurs at x=1a and minima at x=32a

∵ maxima occurs at x=1a=13 ⇒  a=3

Minima occurs at x=32a=12

f12>0⇒ 38+b>0⇒ b>-38

If a<0, then maxima shall occur at x=32a and minima at x=12a

i.e. 32a=13⇒  a=92>0 (not acceptable)

Hence, b>-38

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math><mi>γ</mi></math> be the roots of <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, where <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. Then the value of <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to (where <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mo>⋅</mo></mrow></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer function)
questions.solution:
fx=x3+x2-5x-1
f ′ x =3 x 2 +2x−5= x−1 3x+5

x=-53→ point of local maxima,
x=1→ point of local minima,
Increasing function in -∞,-53∪1,∞
Decreasing function in -53,1

Also, f0=-1,f1=-4,f2=1,f-1=5
f-2=-8+4+10-1=5
f-3=-27+9+15-1=-4
⇒α∈-3,-2; β∈-1,0; γ∈1,2
⇒α=-3, β=-1, γ=1
⇒α+β+γ=-3

questions.q: On which of the following intervals is the function x100+sin x-1 decreasing?
questions.solution:
f x = x 100 +sinx−1

⇒ f ′ x =100 x 99 +cosx

If 0<x< π 2 , then f ′ x >0, , therefore f x is increasing on 0, π 2 .

If 0<x<1, then

100 x 99 >0 and cosx>0 [ ∵x lies between 0 and 1 ]

⇒ f ′ x =100 x 99 +cosx>0

⇒f x is increasing on 0,1 .

If π 2 <x<π, then

100 x 99 >100  ∵x>1⇒ x 99 >1

⇒100 x 99 +cosx>0  ∵cosx≥−1⇒100 x 99 +cosx>99

⇒ f ′ x >0⇒f x is increasing in π 2 ,π .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
For the following questions, choose the correct answers from the codes (a), (b), (c) and (d) defined as follows:
Statement I: ϕx=∫0x3sint+4cost dt,π6,π3. ϕx attains its maximum value at x=π3.
Statement II: ϕx=∫0x3sint+4cost dt, ϕx is increasing function in π6,π3.
options: Statement I is true. Statement II is also true. Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
Statement I is true. Statement II is also true. Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
Statement I is true. Statement II is false.
Statement I is false. Statement II is true.
questions.solution:
ϕ(x)=∫0x(3sint+4cost)dt

Differentiate w.r.t. x,

ϕ'(x)=3sinx+4cosx

ϕ'(x)>0    ∀ x∈ π6,π3

⇒ϕ(x) is an increasing function in interval π6,π3.

⇒ϕ(x) attains maximum value at x=π3.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: For the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>sin</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>cos</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mo>∀</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0,2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, we obtain
options: a local point of maxima at <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi></math>, where <math><mi>α</mi></math> is in <math><msup><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow></msup></math> quadrant

a local point of maxima at <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi></math>, where <math><mi>α</mi></math> is in <math><msup><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mrow></msup></math> quadrant

a local point of minima at <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi></math>, where <math><mi>α</mi></math> is in <math><msup><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow></msup></math> quadrant

a local point of minima at <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi></math>, where <math><mi>α</mi></math> is in <math><msup><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mrow></msup></math> quadrant
questions.solution:
fx=sin⁡x+2cos⁡x, ∀x∈0,2π
f'x=cos⁡x-2sin⁡x
⇒f'x changes its sign from positive to negative at x=tan-112∈0,π2
So at this point, local maxima occurs
⇒f'x changes its sign from negative to positive at x=π+tan-112∈π,3π2
So at this point, local minima occurs
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The function <math><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>22</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>24</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn></math> attains local maximum or minimum at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo> </mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo><</mo><mi>b</mi><mo><</mo><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></math> are in
questions.solution:
dydx=4x3-24x2+44x-24
=4x3-6x2+11x-6
=4x-1x-2x-3
So, dydx=0 at x=1, 2, 3
which are the points of local extremum.
i.e., a=1
b=2
c=3

Hence, a, b, c are in AP
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The maximum area of the rectangle that can be inscribed in a circle of radius $r$ is
questions.solution:
Refer figure
$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{B}=(\mathrm{r} \cos \theta, \mathrm{r} \sin \theta) \\ & \therefore \mathrm{Ab} 2 \mathrm{r} \cos \theta \text { and } \mathrm{BC}=2 \mathrm{r} \sin \theta \\ & \text { Area }(\mathrm{ABCD})=\mathrm{AB} \times \mathrm{BC} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{f}(\theta)=(2 \mathrm{r} \cos \theta)(2 \mathrm{r} \sin \theta)=2 \mathrm{r}^2 \sin 2 \theta \\ & \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\theta)=4 \mathrm{r}^2 \cos 2 \theta \text { and when } \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\theta)=0 \text {, we write } \\ & \cos 2 \theta=0 \Rightarrow \cos 2 \theta=\cos \frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ & \mathrm{f}^{\prime \prime}(\theta)=-8 \mathrm{r}^2 \sin 2 \theta \text { and } \mathrm{f}^{\prime \prime}(\theta)_{\left[\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}\right]}=-8 \mathrm{r}^2 < 0 \backslash \end{aligned} $$ Thus, area of required rectangle is maximum when $\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore \mathrm{AB}=2 \mathrm{r} \cos \frac{\pi}{4}=2 \mathrm{r}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{r} \text { and } \\ & \mathrm{BC}=2 \mathrm{r} \sin \frac{\pi}{4}=2 \mathrm{r}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{r} \end{aligned} $$ $\therefore$ Maximum Area $(\mathrm{ABCD})==(\sqrt{2} \mathrm{r})(\sqrt{2} \mathrm{r})=2 \mathrm{r}^2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>k</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>k</mi></math> neither touches nor intercepts the curve <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>ln</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></math>, then minimum value of <math><mi>k</mi><mo>∈</mo></math> is
questions.solution:
fx=-1+kx+k

fx represents straight line with slope k and y-intercept k-1.

fx+1=kx+1

So for all values of k, it always passes through -1,-1

For minimum slope of line, it will be touching the curve y=lnx

When two curves touches, then at the common point their slopes are equal.

f'x=k , dydx=1x

k=1x ⇒ x=1k, y=ln1k

common point ⇒ 1k, -lnk

Slope of line can be written from two points on the line.-lnk--11k--1=k

1-lnk=k1k+1

=1+k

lnk+k=0

For solution of above equation, consider fk=lnk+k

f1e=ln1e+1e=1e-1

f1e<0

f1e=ln1e+1e=1e-12=2-e2e

f1e>0

So f1e·f1e<0

So atleast one root lies between 1eand 1e.

Hence, k∈1e, 1e
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mstyle displaystyle="false"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>x</mi></mfrac></mstyle></msup></math> is:
questions.solution:
Let y=x1x⇒logy=1xlogx

⇒1ydydx=1x2-logexx2

⇒dydx=x1x1-logexx2

For 1<x<∞, x1x>0

and 1-logexx2>0 in 1, e

and 1-logexx2<0 in e, ∞

Hence, f(x) is increasing in (1, e) and decreasing in (e, ∞).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
